postingDefaults.cadence: 3 shorts/week for 14 days, then double down on winners
approvalChecklist: No copyrighted footage/audio unless licensed or generated in-house
No fake stats, fake credentials, or fabricated claims
Upload as private first, review on YouTube Studio, then schedule/public

channels.thesis: Practical AI-agent workflows are exploding, but most creators stay abstract. Win with concrete demos, failure modes, and tool comparisons for operators.
channels.targetAudience: founders, operators, engineers, and creators who want practical AI agent workflows
sampleTopics: I gave an AI agent one business task and graded the receipts
The 5-step approval gate every autonomous agent needs
What happens when a coding agent ships without tests
riskNotes: Avoid fake product claims; verify live tool behavior before posting

channels.thesis: People want market-implied odds explained simply. Use charts and concise narration, with strict educational disclaimers.
channels.targetAudience: curious investors, data nerds, prediction-market users, and business news watchers
sampleTopics: This market says the consensus is wrong
How prediction markets price a surprise before headlines
The simplest way to read implied probability
riskNotes: Educational only; not financial advice; cite market data source and timestamp

channels.thesis: Teachers need time-saving workflows, not generic AI hype. Create practical classroom organization and content-prep videos for educators.
channels.targetAudience: elementary teachers, teacher-creators, and education entrepreneurs
sampleTopics: A 10-minute system for Monday lesson prep
Turn one worksheet into a week of warmups
The teacher inbox triage workflow I would automate first
riskNotes: Audience is teachers, not children; avoid collecting student data or showing real student info
